fields.name: Md Mursalatul Islam Pallob
fields.roles: Software Developer,AI/ML Researcher,Competitive Programmer,Full-Stack Engineer,Problem Solver
fields.about_text: I'm a passionate Computer Science graduate from Dhaka International University with a strong interest in AI/ML research and full-stack development. From building real-time emotion recognition systems to competing at ICPC, I thrive at the intersection of cutting-edge research and practical engineering. I've led programming clubs, mentored hundreds of competitive programmers, and contributed to research accepted by Taylor & Francis. When I'm not coding, I'm probably thinking about new ideas to bring to life.

fields.title: Speech Emotion Recognition in Real-time
fields.description: Developed a real-time Speech Emotion Recognition system using an LSTM-based deep learning model in Keras to analyze live microphone input and identify expressed emotions. Prepared and augmented audio datasets (RAVDESS, TESS), extracted acoustic features and spectrogram representations. Delivered a real-time inference pipeline achieving 87% accuracy on benchmark evaluations with silence detection for robustness.

fields.title: Infant Cry Analysis Web Application
fields.description: Full-stack web application using Django and PostgreSQL for detection and multi-class diagnosis of infant distress from cry recordings. Integrated a machine learning backend capable of processing Spectro-Derivative audio features to classify cries into distress categories (pain, hunger, sleepy, healthy). Designed a responsive Bootstrap frontend for classification results and audio feature visualizations.

fields.title: Infant Cry Analysis ML Models
fields.description: Designed and implemented a Balanced Augmented Spectro-Derivative Ensemble Framework for multi-class infant distress diagnosis with high classification accuracy. Extracted and analyzed spectrograms and Spectro-Derivative features from infant cry audio for feature engineering. Developed and evaluated ensemble machine learning models for robust performance across different datasets.

fields.title: Suicidal Ideation Prediction
fields.description: Built an end-to-end NLP pipeline in Python for preprocessing text data, tokenization, and feature extraction to support supervised classification of suicidal ideation from short-form text. Implemented and compared transformer-based fine-tuning and classical ML baselines. Packaged experiments in reproducible notebooks with documented ethical/privacy considerations.

Developed and implemented machine learning models for voice and emotion stress analysis.
Collaborated closely with Flutter, DevOps, and UX/UI engineers to integrate AI-driven features into mobile apps, aligning with project goals.

fields.bullets:
Oversaw club activities, including organizing coding competitions, workshops, and hackathons.
Collaborated with the executive team to create opportunities for members to advance their programming knowledge.
Organized DIU CSE Fall Fest 2024.

Managed diverse programming contests and authored 59 original competitive programming problems.
Mentored club members in competitive programming, preparing teams for ICPC and IUPC.
Organized seminars and fundraising events to facilitate student branch initiatives.
